# UC-14.4.18 · IoT Device Provisioning Audit

## Description

Unauthorized provisioning events create shadow devices and billing abuse.

## Value

Unauthorized provisioning events create shadow devices and billing abuse.

## Implementation

Compare provisioning rate to approved baseline. Alert on new template or IAM principal. Cross-check with HR for contractor access. For AWS, prefer CloudTrail (`sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`) with `eventSource="iot.amazonaws.com"` (and relevant `eventName` values) instead of a non-standard `cloudtrail:iot` sourcetype; keep `iot:provisioning` only if that is your normalized pipeline.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: AWS IoT Fleet / Azure DPS audit.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: AWS CloudTrail (`sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`; filter `eventSource` / `eventName` for IoT control-plane APIs such as `iot.amazonaws.com`), or a custom `iot:provisioning` sourcetype from your pipeline..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Compare provisioning rate to approved baseline. Alert on new template or IAM principal. Cross-check with HR for contractor access. For AWS, prefer CloudTrail (`sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`) with `eventSource="iot.amazonaws.com"` (and relevant `eventName` values) instead of a non-standard `cloudtrail:iot` sourcetype; keep `iot:provisioning` only if that is your normalized pipeline.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=audit sourcetype="iot:provisioning"
| where action IN ("RegisterThing","CreateCertificate","AttachPolicy")
| stats count by actor, device_template, src
| where count > 50
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**IoT Device Provisioning Audit** — Unauthorized provisioning events create shadow devices and billing abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: AWS CloudTrail (`sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`; filter `eventSource` / `eventName` for IoT control-plane APIs such as `iot.amazonaws.com`), or a custom `iot:provisioning` sourcetype from your pipeline. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AWS IoT Fleet / Azure DPS audit. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: audit; **sourcetype**: iot:provisioning. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=audit, sourcetype="iot:provisioning". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where action IN ("RegisterThing","CreateCertificate","AttachPolicy")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by actor, device_template, src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare device counts, last-seen times, and health fields to the IoT platform console (for example AWS IoT, Azure IoT Hub, or Google Cloud IoT) for the same period. Verify API polling intervals and that offline rules match your operational definition.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (provisioning events), Timeline (bursts), Bar chart (by actor).

## SPL

```spl
index=audit sourcetype="iot:provisioning"
| where action IN ("RegisterThing","CreateCertificate","AttachPolicy")
| stats count by actor, device_template, src
| where count > 50
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (provisioning events), Timeline (bursts), Bar chart (by actor).
